steps.title: Track the passage turn
steps.content: The passage says historians of capitalism often imply capitalism was external to Africa. It then uses Africanist scholarship to show why that can mislead, and the final sentence explains the likely reason: limited archival economic data for precolonial Africa.

steps.title: Choose the full purpose
steps.content: Choice A narrows the issue to defining capitalism. Choice C makes Africanist methods the focus, and Choice D invents a debate about interpreting archival data. The answer is B.
